items.title: Aboriginal artist recreating 'oldest living weaving practice' with glass

children.content: Lower Southern Arrernte woman Jenni Kemarre Martiniello creates glass artworks by combining a centuries-old Italian glassmaking technique with millennia-old Indigenous weaving practices.

gallery.altText: A person's hands working on a woven-patterned glass sculpture with a blowtorch.

items.title: ACT signs deal for CSIRO site, plans to build 3,000 homes

children.content: After being flagged for development over a decade ago, the ACT government has reached an agreement for the sale of land at a former CSIRO agriculture research site to construction of thousands new homes.

image.altText: A drone shot of green pastures

items.title: 'He gave us 10 extra years': Australian-first immune centre opens in Canberra

children.content: The Centre for Personalised Medicine at Canberra Hospital uses cutting-edge technology to identify the causes of complex immune conditions to tailor treatments for patients.

image.altText: An elderly man and his adult daughter smiling outside.

items.title: The story of Sidney Leonard Morse had been lost to history — until now

children.content: Captain Sidney Leonard Morse's achievements would have remained hidden were it not for a chance discovery by a volunteer historian collating family trees at the Australian War Memorial.

gallery.altText: A Military Cross medal in front of a line of other military medals.

items.title: Canberra paramedics become patients every couple of days

children.content: ACT Ambulance Service recorded more than 190 occupational violence incidents last financial year.

image.altText: Mr Dutton smiles in his uniform, standing in front of the logo for the ACT Emergency Services Agency.

items.title: ADFA cadet facing 79 extra charges after hidden camera found in bathroom

children.content: Police allege Tain Stephens was living at the Australian Defence Force Academy in Canberra when he hid a camera inside a soap dispenser in a female bathroom to capture intimate recordings of women.

image.altText: A man with short hair wearing a t-shirt walks out of a court building.

items.title: Year 11 and 12 students should be forced to study maths, review says

children.content: A committee has found students in the ACT should study maths through to the end of year 12 to ensure they have better long-term outcomes, arguing a lack of maths understanding can exacerbate social inequality.

gallery.altText: A generic photo of a student writing on a pad.

thumbnail.altText: A generic photo of a student writing on a pad.

items.title: World Cup contends with heat in test case for sport

items.title: 'Key perpetrator' of spreading antisemitism draws ire of royal commission

children.content: It was gloves off at the royal commission on antisemitism this week, with Elon Musk's X bearing the brunt of the inquiry's frustrations with obstructive social media giants.

image.altText: A YouTube female executive on the left, a Meta executive in the middle, and Elon Musk on the right in a graphic.

items.title: 'Sacred' Coorong wetlands bracing for impact of bird flu

children.content: South Australia's Coorong is a sanctuary for birds and marine life but it is increasingly under threat with the arrival of bird flu in Australia the latest in a rolling barrage of crises.

items.title: Hinch revealed a scenario that seemed too crazy to be true — but he was right

children.content: Derryn Hinch was not a hero to every survivor of sexual abuse, but in the decades when they lacked allies, he was their fiercest public advocate.

items.title: Police release video of killer Bradley Murdoch filmed weeks before his death

children.content: In never-before-seen footage of police questioning Bradley John Murdoch in his final weeks, the notorious murderer repeatedly tells officers he "knows nothing" about the location of Peter Falconio's remains.

items.title: Broken Hill's long legacy of lead exposure

items.title: How Jessica Mauboy is marking NAIDOC Week with her daughter

children.content: From singing to her daughter to laughing with her aunties, Jessica Mauboy is all about family. This NAIDOC Week, the star is sharing the music, culture and community she grew up with in a whole new way.

items.title: Story of family 'terrorised by bogeyman' gets modern update in Cape Fear

children.content: The 1957 book The Executioners has been made into two movies and an iconic episode of The Simpsons. It's now a TV show, starring Amy Adams, Patrick Wilson and Javier Bardem.

items.title: Hobart's Mona announces plans to set up art museum in Thailand

children.content: Museum of Old and New Art founder David Walsh signs an agreement to set up MONA Bangkok in Thailand. Mona says the new iteration will be "unconventional and unique", like its Hobart museum.

items.title: Shock resignation of NT children's commissioner deemed 'a tragedy'

children.content: Australia's peak advocate for Aboriginal children has described the circumstances around child protection in the Northern Territory as "dire", following the shock resignation of the Territory's children's commissioner.

items.title: The 99-year-old Victorian mechanic on the verge of a world record

items.title: That's life: Derryn Hinch's most memorable media moments

items.title: Sinner, Zverev to face off in blockbuster Wimbledon final

children.content: British wildcard Arthur Fery's bid to make Wimbledon history has ended after he was knocked out by Alexander Zverev, who will face off against reigning champion Jannik Sinner in the final.

items.title: Workers waiting a year for entitlements from GFG may never be paid

children.content: Jun Samonte has spent 14 years working for the same Whyalla business. He now fears he will never receive the $53,000 he is owed in entitlements, despite already waiting a year.

items.title: Grandmother's 32-hour bus 'adventure' home after train shutdown

children.content: A bus trip that should have taken only 13 hours turned into a 32-hour interstate marathon for a Torquay grandmother due to this week's Telstra outage, while there is still no date for when Victorian passengers can claim compensation from V/Line.
